heading: Enlistment to serve against United States
label: 2390 Enlistment to serve against United States
text: Whoever enlists or is engaged within the United States or in any place subject to the jurisdiction thereof, with intent to serve in armed hostility against the United States, shall be fined under this title or imprisoned not more than three years, or both.1See 1994 Amendment note below.1
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 8 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 835 Stat. 1089Mandatory punishment provision was rephrased in the alternative.Minor changes were made in phraseology.

Amendments1994—, which directed the amendment of this section by striking “fined not more than $100” and inserting “fined under this title”, was executed by substituting “fined under this title” for “fined $100”, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 103–322
